questions.statement: The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act of 2011 added which of the following Directive Principle of State Policy to the Constitution?
options: <span style="">To secure opportunities for healthy development of children</span> /
<span style="">To protect monuments, places, and objects of artistic or historic interest that are declared to be of national importance</span> /
<span style="">Participation of workers in the management of industries</span> /

questions.statement: Which of the following Articles deals with the improvement of public health under the Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution?&nbsp;&nbsp;
Which one of the following is<strong> NOT</strong> correctly matched?
options: Article 39 A - Equal Justice and free legal aid /
Article 48 - Separation of Judiciary from Executive /

questions.statement: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution of India were borrowed from the _________ Constitution.
The constitution of India declares that&nbsp;Directive Principles of State Policies (DPSPs) are fundamental to the governance of the country because
options: They lay down principles that help in attaining political democracy. /
They lay down principles that ensure economic democracy and establish a welfare state. /
They lay down instructions that promote the welfare of the individuals. /
They are supplementary to the Fundamental rights of the citizens.&nbsp; /
